question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The actual CPI increase for March was 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for the market to resolve YES. Since the official data is already available and shows CPI increased by only 0.3%, there is zero probability the market resolves YES.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased 0.3% (seasonally adjusted)
0.3% actual increase is well below the 0.9% threshold
Data was released April 10, 2026, making the outcome certain
Market closes April 10, 2026, same day as data release
risks: Data revision risk (though CPI revisions are typically small)
Potential confusion about seasonal vs non-seasonal adjustment
Market resolution timing relative to data release
